content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 28, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on Jun 28, 2026, focused on stabilizing the seamless cloud agent handoff, hardening multi-tenant cloud security, and resolving critical production regressions. Significant progress was made in orchestrator reliability, vision engine compatibility, and cloud infrastructure deployment.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

### Seamless Cloud Agent Handoff
- Implemented a robust handoff flow that provisions dedicated agents alongside shared ones, ensuring zero message loss during the switch window via outgoing message queuing ([#9843](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9843), [#9844](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9844)).
- Enabled a visually seamless, "invisible" re-point mechanism that prevents UI flashes and draft loss, followed by the automatic deletion of transient shared bridges upon successful handoff ([#9845](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9845), [#9846](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9846)).
- Added end-to-end success verification for the handoff flow in the cloud test harness ([#9856](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9856)).

### Cloud Infrastructure & Security Hardening
- Implemented critical security measures including IP-pinned `safeFetch` to prevent SSRF, reserved environment variable denylists, and fail-closed isolated database provisioning ([#9864](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9864), [#9858](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9858), [#9859](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9859)).
- Added rate-limiting backstops and sybil-prevention guards for free-grant signups, alongside a runtime kill-switch for cloud feature flags ([#9863](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9863), [#9866](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9866)).
- Improved observability by making stale provisioning-daemon heartbeats "loud" and enforcing public URL reachability for successful deployments ([#9871](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9871), [#9870](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9870)).

### Orchestrator & Agent Stability
- Resolved production issues where dedicated agents defaulted to local inference by forcing correct cloud topology and pre-seeding configuration ([#9905](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9905), [#9903](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9903)).
- Fixed orchestrator build-relay issues to prevent duplicate message posting and improved sub-agent failure evaluation ([#9855](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9855), [#9902](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9902)).
- Updated `PaddleOcrService` to support PaddleOCR 3.x and removed dead distillation scripts to clean up the training pipeline ([#9924](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9924), [#9921](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9921)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
- A wide range of PRs were opened to support the ongoing repo restructure and performance optimizations, including enabling inference hot-path caching in staging ([#9929](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9929)), fixing cache pagination ([#9928](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9928)), and refining orchestrator/agent communication ([#9901](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9901), [#9900](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9900)).

## CLOSED ISSUES

### Cloud & Auth Stability
- Resolved a critical production crash on the OAuth authorize page by correctly mounting `StewardProvider` ([#9881](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9881)).
- Addressed a persistent issue where dedicated cloud agents incorrectly fell back to local inference due to misconfigured cloud topology ([#9887](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9887)).

## NEW ISSUES

### Performance & Architecture
- Identified a significant latency bottleneck in dedicated-agent chat caused by cloud-api pre-forward processing ([#9899](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9899)).
- Opened a series of "umbrella" issues to address technical debt, including the need for better testing coverage, role-based security models, and improved TUI/GUI view contracts ([#9940](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9940), [#9943](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9943), [#9948](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9948), [#9946](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/9946)).

## ACTIVE ISSUES

- **[#9853] Cloud apps hardening:** Extensive discussion on multi-tenant security. Recent audits confirmed that while the architecture is solid, defense-in-depth improvements are needed for sybil-prevention and CI-gated tenant isolation.
- **[#9887] Dedicated agent inference failure:** A deep-dive investigation revealed that while the worker correctly writes the agent config, the agent-side topology resolver was failing to load the `deploymentTarget` correctly. Fixes are being consolidated to ensure the agent correctly identifies as a cloud runtime.
- **[#9874] Planner confabulation:** Structural fixes were implemented to suppress tool-promotion during bot-to-bot crosstalk and improve context preservation for reacted messages. Verification tests are passing, and the issue is recommended for closure.
- **[#9880] Wake word verification:** Extensive verification evidence was added for Linux-native and Pixel 9a devices, confirming the "hey eliza" wake word performance. Discussion continues on potential activation-gate tuning to reduce false positives.
